evals.prompt: I'm adding runic to my FalkorDB-backed Python app. The graph is called 'socialnet' and runs on localhost:6379. Walk me through the full setup: init the environment, configure env.py, create a first migration that adds a range index on User.email and a unique constraint, and apply it.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step showing: runic init, correct env.py with FALKORDB_URL/FALKORDB_GRAPH, runic revision -m '...', a complete migration file with correct upgrade/downgrade (constraint after index in upgrade, constraint before index in downgrade), and runic upgrade head.

assertions.name: mentions-runic-init
assertions.description: Response mentions `runic init` to scaffold the directory

assertions.name: env-py-has-socialnet
assertions.description: env.py example references 'socialnet' as graph_name or FALKORDB_GRAPH

assertions.name: migration-has-create-range-index
assertions.description: Migration upgrade body calls op.create_range_index for User.email

assertions.name: migration-has-create-constraint
assertions.description: Migration upgrade body calls op.create_constraint for User email

assertions.name: downgrade-drops-constraint-before-index
assertions.description: Migration downgrade drops the constraint before dropping the range index

assertions.name: mentions-runic-upgrade-head
assertions.description: Response shows `runic upgrade head` to apply the migration

evals.prompt: I need to rename the property 'username' to 'handle' on all Person nodes in my FalkorDB graph. This migration is one-way — I can't reconstruct the old data. Write the runic migration file and tell me which flags to set.
evals.expected_output: A complete migration file with irreversible=True, snapshot=True, upgrade using op.rename_property('Person', 'username', 'handle'), downgrade raising NotImplementedError or clearly explaining the snapshot restore mechanism.

assertions.name: downgrade-raises-or-explains
assertions.description: downgrade() raises an error or clearly states this is irreversible / uses snapshot restore

assertions.name: correct-property-names
assertions.description: Property names are 'username' (old) and 'handle' (new), not reversed

evals.prompt: My team has two branches each with a new runic migration based on the same head revision. 'runic heads' shows two entries: abc123 and def456. How do I resolve this and what does the merge revision file look like?
evals.expected_output: Explains runic merge abc123 def456 -m '...', shows the resulting merge revision file with down_revision as a tuple ('abc123', 'def456'), and explains runic upgrade head to apply it.

assertions.name: uses-runic-merge-command
assertions.description: Response shows the `runic merge abc123 def456 -m "..."` command

assertions.name: down-revision-is-tuple
assertions.description: Merge revision file shows down_revision as a tuple, e.g. ("abc123", "def456")

assertions.name: mentions-upgrade-head-after-merge
assertions.description: Explains to run `runic upgrade head` after creating the merge revision

assertions.name: does-not-suggest-editing-files
assertions.description: Does NOT suggest manually editing existing migration files as the resolution strategy
